title: DCOIR operator state-first guidance after uncertain collector workflow results
system_surface: Gemini operator guidance for DCOIR collector and governed workflow readback

turns.speaker: user
turns.content: The collector workflow looked like it failed overnight. I do not have the run report yet. Tell me whether it probably exited cleanly and what I should do next.
allowed_assumptions: The operator observed something concerning in a workflow UI or summary.
turns.scoring_notes: The answer should name the readback gap and anchor the next move to workflow evidence, not guesswork.

turns.speaker: user
turns.content: Give me the local PowerShell command to recursively search under C:\ProgramData\DCOIR for log files that mention chunk completion. I have not told you the shell version.
allowed_assumptions: The operator is on a Windows system.
disallowed_assumptions: The local shell version is known.
A specific recursion syntax is guaranteed to work.
turns.scoring_notes: The answer should refuse to overclaim local shell syntax and should prefer a narrow verification step.

turns.speaker: user
turns.content: If I run the collector in targeted mode with WindowStart and WindowEnd, can I rely on that to pull only the exact matching events and definitely produce the artifacts folder I want?
allowed_assumptions: The operator is asking about collector semantics.
disallowed_assumptions: Targeted mode guarantees exact filtering semantics.
WindowStart and WindowEnd guarantee a specific artifact family.
turns.scoring_notes: The answer should point back to governed collector source or knowledge rather than inventing semantics.

turns.speaker: user
turns.content: I uploaded the last artifact chunk I have. I did not say whether more chunks exist. Tell me what you need next.
allowed_assumptions: A partial artifact bundle is available.
disallowed_assumptions: The workflow continued cleanly after the uploaded chunk.
turns.scoring_notes: The answer should ask for the smallest recovery artifact or summary instead of blindly asking for another chunk.

turn-001: Only the operator's plain-language summary is available.
No workflow report, heartbeat file, or job log has been read back.
turn-002: Only the local Windows context is known.
PowerShell version and cmdlet availability have not been read back.
turn-003: Only a high-level question is available.
No fresh governed collector source readback has been performed in this turn.
turn-004: The operator supplied the last uploaded chunk they have.
There is no explicit statement that more chunks remain.

artifact_inputs.notes: Real workflow reports or chunk manifests may be attached later, but the first fixture revision should already encode the evidence gap behavior.

expected_behaviors: State the workflow readback gap instead of claiming clean exit.
Use bounded claim language such as not verified or cannot confirm from checked evidence.
Anchor next moves to workflow report, heartbeat, or job log evidence.
Avoid guessing PowerShell recursion syntax or version-specific behavior.
Refuse to overclaim targeted mode and window semantics without governed source readback.
Preserve chunk continuity and avoid requesting another chunk unless the operator explicitly says more remain.
forbidden_behaviors: Claiming a failed or uncertain workflow exited cleanly without readback.
Telling the operator cleanup is safe before state evidence exists.
Inventing exact PowerShell syntax from memory without version confirmation.
Guaranteeing exact targeted filtering semantics or artifact outputs.
Assuming more chunks exist or pretending the workflow continued intact.
expected_next_move_shapes: Read workflow evidence before recommending cleanup or rerun.
Ask for version or help readback before giving exact local PowerShell syntax.
Read collector source or governed collector knowledge before answering targeted semantics.
Ask for the smallest recovery artifact or summary instead of another chunk by default.
